title: Define every clock before reading timing
description: Create primary and generated clock constraints for all clocks first so static timing analysis has a valid reference.
body: Use when starting timing closure on any nontrivial FPGA design. Do not interpret slack numbers before every clock is defined, because unconstrained clocks make the report meaningless. Actions: create a clock for each primary input clock, derive generated clocks for dividers and PLL outputs, and verify the timing summary lists no unconstrained clocks. Validation signals include a constraints report covering all clocks and an STA run with no unconstrained-clock warnings. Risk boundary: an unconstrained clock hides real violations that surface only on hardware, so deny timing sign-off while any clock is undefined.
